formats.format: $timestamp=timestamp$ Manch SIEM: Alert - $alert_name$ Severity: $severity$ Source: $src_ip=ipv4$ Target: $dst_ip=ipv4$ $description$
$timestamp=timestamp$ Manch SIEM: Compliance - $rule_name$ Status: $status$ Asset: $asset$ User: $user$
$timestamp=timestamp$ Manch SIEM: Auth Event - User $user$ $result$ from $src_ip=ipv4$ Method: $auth_method$
$timestamp=timestamp$ Manch SIEM: File $action$ - $file_path$ by $user$ on $hostname$ from $src_ip=ipv4$

metadata_details.purpose: Parser for Manch SIEM logs using JSON parsing to normalise events into the AI-SIEM schema.
metadata_details.dependency_summary: Requires ingestion of Manch SIEM security event logs.
metadata_details.performance_impact: Minimal – this parser processes JSON formatted SIEM logs.
metadata_details.tags: manch, siem, logs, json, parser, security
metadata_details.author: Kenny Cho

analysis.semantic_summary: Parser processes Manch SIEM security logs across four main categories: security detections, compliance findings, authentication events, and file activity monitoring. It standardizes these events into a normalized format with consistent timestamp handling and IPv4 address parsing.
optimization_opportunities: Add input validation for severity levels
Implement field normalization for user fields
Add error handling for malformed timestamps
analysis.lua_code:
-- SentinelOne Parser: manch_siem_logs-latest 
-- OCSF Class: Detection Finding (2001)
-- Performance Level: High-volume optimized
-- Generated: 2025-10-13T12:28:20.975138

-- Pre-compile regex patterns for performance
local timestamp_pattern = "^%d%d%d%d%-%d%d%-%d%dT%d%d:%d%d:%d%d"
local ipv4_pattern = "^%d+%.%d+%.%d+%.%d+$"

-- Cached severity mappings
local severity_map = {
    ["1"] = "INFO",
    ["2"] = "LOW", 
    ["3"] = "MEDIUM",
    ["4"] = "HIGH",
    ["5"] = "CRITICAL"
}

function transform(record)
    -- Input validation with early return
    if not record or type(record) ~= "table" then
        return nil, "Invalid input record"
    end

    -- Initialize OCSF-compliant output with local vars
    local output = {
        class_uid = 2001,
        class_name = "Detection Finding",
        category_uid = 2,
        category_name = "Findings",
        activity_id = 1,
        type_uid = 200101,
        metadata = {
            product = {
                name = "Manch SIEM",
                vendor_name = "Manch"
            },
            version = "1.0.0"
        },
        time = 0,
        src_endpoint = {},
        dst_endpoint = {},
        user = {}
    }

    -- Timestamp handling with validation
    if record.timestamp then
        local ts = record.timestamp
        if string.match(ts, timestamp_pattern) then
            -- Convert to UNIX milliseconds
            local year, month, day, hour, min, sec = 
                string.match(ts, "(%d+)-(%d+)-(%d+)T(%d+):(%d+):(%d+)")
            if year then
                output.time = os.time({
                    year = tonumber(year),
                    month = tonumber(month),
                    day = tonumber(day),
                    hour = tonumber(hour),
                    min = tonumber(min),
                    sec = tonumber(sec)
                }) * 1000
            end
        end
    end

    -- IP address handling with validation
    if record.src_ip and string.match(record.src_ip, ipv4_pattern) then
        output.src_endpoint.ip = record.src_ip
    end
    
    if record.dst_ip and string.match(record.dst_ip, ipv4_pattern) then
        output.dst_endpoint.ip = record.dst_ip
    end

    -- User field mapping with sanitization
    if record.user then
        output.user.name = string.gsub(record.user, "[^%w%s%-_@%.]", "")
    end

    -- Alert details mapping
    if record.alert_name then
        output.finding = {
            title = record.alert_name,
            desc = record.description or "No description provided"
        }
    end

    -- Severity mapping
    if record.severity then
        output.severity = severity_map[record.severity] or "UNKNOWN"
    end

    -- Status handling for compliance events
    if record.status then
        output.status = string.upper(record.status)
    end

    -- Validation of required fields
    if not output.time or output.time == 0 then
        output.time = os.time() * 1000
    end

    if not output.class_uid or output.class_uid == 0 then
        return nil, "Invalid class_uid"
    end

    return output
end

-- Error handling wrapper
function safe_transform(record)
    local status, result = pcall(transform, record)
    if status then
        return result
    else
        return nil, string.format("Transform error: %s", result)
    end
end

setup_steps: Deploy LUA module to approved directory
